summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a GPG release signing feature, then extend it with a user-facing verification script and document the two-phase release workflow.
summary.outcome: All review threads resolved across multiple rounds; added scripts/verify-release.sh fetching the maintainer's key from pen.so/gpg.asc; updated release-verification.md and CLAUDE.md with the full release workflow.

repo: Release workflow is two-phase: CI builds and publishes assets first, then the maintainer separately runs gpg-sign-release.sh to sign — Claude must not sign immediately when asked to 'create a release'.
The project uses a worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/gpg-sign-releases-92GMZ for PR branch work.
GPG key is published at https://pen.so/gpg.asc — this is the canonical source for key import in verification docs and scripts.
PR review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL API, not the REST API.

code.finding: Using grep to filter gpg --import output breaks under set -euo pipefail when GPG emits lines about new signatures/subkeys that don't match — fixed by replacing grep filter with || true to show all output.
SHA256 verification was silently skipped when no checksum file existed; clobber of existing .asc files was silent — both needed explicit warnings/notices.

workflow: When checking for new PR comments, query both review threads (GraphQL for unresolved) and issue-level comments separately — they are different API surfaces.
Checking comment timestamps against last push time is the reliable way to detect genuinely new feedback vs already-addressed threads.

friction: The user had to ask 'fix new comments' four times across the session; two of those rounds found nothing new, suggesting the PR comment-checking workflow needed refinement before confirming the PR was clean.
greptile review posted multiple rounds of comments requiring repeated polling cycles.
open_items: CLAUDE.md documents the two-phase release workflow but the actual gpg-sign-release.sh invocation still needs to be deferred until after CI publishes assets — this is now documented but relies on Claude following the instructions.
verify-release.sh --checksums flag for SHA256 verification is optional and not enforced — users could skip integrity checks silently.
